title: Positive definite functions as uniformly ergodic multipliers of the Fourier algebra
summary: Let G be a locally compact group and let $φ$ be a positive definite function on G with $φ(e)=1$. This function defines a multiplication operator $M_φ$ on the Fourier algebra $A(G)$ of $G$. The aim of this paper is to classify the ergodic properties of the operators $M_φ$, focusing on several key factors, including the subgroup $H_φ=\{x\in G\colon φ(x)=1\}$, the spectrum of $M_φ$, or how ``spread-out'' a power of $M_φ$ can be. We show that the multiplication operator $M_φ$ is uniformly mean ergodic if and only if $H_φ$ is open and 1 is not an accumulation point of the spectrum of $M_φ$. Equivalently, this happens when some power of $φ$ is not far, in the multiplier norm, from a function supported on finitely many cosets of $H_φ$. Additionally, we show that the powers of $M_φ$ converge in norm if, and only if, the operator is uniformly mean ergodic and $H_φ=\{x\in G\colon |φ(x)|=1\}$.
